content:
AS you mention AOL and have more than one account - please all the information at this link :

  • https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-and-yahoo


re :it didn't ask for a password

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • scroll to Passwords section
  • click on Saved Passwords
  • Click on 'Show Passwords

It will display all the accounts. All accounts may display 2 lines for each email address = username, one incoming (either 'mailbox:// for pop accounts or 'imap://' for imap account) and one outgoing. OR you may see a line called 'oauth2://' where the password is very long and created by the server,

You need to remove all lines for her account. Then Exit Thunderbird - allow a few moments for background processes to complete then restart Thunderbird.

content:
re :the setup asks for the password but it meant the password from the emailprovider to allow thirdpartyapps access.


You would already have an existing email address with an email provider. It might be an email address provided by your internet service provider or a free one provided by eg: gmail.

You would be able to logon to that email address webmail account via a browser to directly access the account. You would have to provide your email address and password in order to access the webmail account. This is the only place where you can modify any password. The password would never be 'provided by the email provider' because that's totally insecure. The password would be created by the person who owns the account - the person who has the email address assigned to them. They create and modify their own password.

All email client programs are third party apps. Thunderbird is an email client program you install on your computer.

When you are creating a mail account in Thunderbird, it means you set up server settings to access the server to download emails into the mail account for a particular email address.

It requires the email address and password for the account to be set up. That password is the one created by the account holder of the email address and it is the sae password they would use to access their webmail account to view emails directly on email providers website.

That email address and password is sent to the server that currently stores all your emails to verify you want Thunderbird to access your account and to allow download of emails.

If you are setting up an account which uses Authentication Method : Oauth2 (gmail. outlook and yahoo servers use it), then you may get a prompt from Gmail or Yahoo or Office365 to confirm you really are you and to set up to allow Thunderbird access. That means you are being asked for email and password again.

There is no special different password just to allow third party access. There is only one password. It is the password the server accepts for access to that email address.

However, If the user has set up additional 2FA (2 Factor Authentication) also known as 2 step verification, then they may get a prompt on their mobile for the key also known as passkey, but that is not Thunderbird.

eg: Yahoo - https://help.yahoo.com/kb/-step-verification-security-key-sln35380.html eg: gmail - https://support.google.com/mail/answer/185839
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
It seems you googled and selected the link that auto opens on the donate page before you had even downloaded the program. So you made a kind donation, thankyou. Then you performed the download and got the donate page for a second time.

This only occurred because you selected the donation link first.

Normally, to get a download from official website, you would go to this link: https://www.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/all/

After the download, Thunderbird may pop up the please donate link because it does not make huge charges for software like other companies. It relies on people making a donation to help with development.

Not a scam. Thunderbird does ask for a donation after a download. You just happened to select the donation page from a google search before you had downloaded, so ended up seeing it twice  :)
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
re : Whenever I click download

How many times do you need to download Thunderbird? Usually people only need to do it the once unless they are setting up Thunderbird on several computers.

Please understand, there is no knowledge whether someone has donated or not when they click on download button. It's an auto action on website to display the donation request when the 'download' button is selected.
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
re :Thunderbird No download

Are you saying you are clicking on 'Download' button and not getting a download ?

When you click on 'Download' , the file should be downloaded to your computer. I've got a Windows 11 OS and all downloads go to my User Account 'Downloads' folder.

I then access my 'Downloads' folder to see what was downloaded. Then click on the thunderbird setup exe file to start installation.

Have you checked to see what is in your 'Downloads' folder ?

Use this link to get download: https://www.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/all/

Do make sure you selected the correct options for you eg: Language
creator: Toad-Hall

content: I assumed, incorrectly, that as the download finished and the Donation window opened "it" was the download. I then (eventually!) opened my eyes and saw the download was called Thunderbird Setup 152.0(13).exe. I clicked it and all was OK!! But clicking the "Download" button always takes me to the Donation page, which is quite confusing, whilst the actual download performs quietly without indication that it is happening. I now have Thunderbird up and running, and thank you for the quick response.
